questions.stem: This two-panel figure contrasts tumor immunity with and without antibodies directed against CTLA-4 and PD-1/PD-L1, showing restored cytotoxic T cell killing when the antibodies are used. What general class of cancer therapy does this illustrate?

explanation.summary: Blocking inhibitory checkpoint pathways (CTLA-4 and PD-1/PD-L1) with antibodies removes the brakes on T cells, restoring their ability to activate and kill tumor cells. This checkpoint blockade approach is approved for advanced solid tumors and some lymphomas.
explanation.whyCorrect: Blocking inhibitory checkpoint pathways (CTLA-4 and PD-1/PD-L1) with antibodies removes the brakes on T cells, restoring their ability to activate and kill tumor cells. This checkpoint blockade approach is approved for advanced solid tumors and some lymphomas.

questions.stem: This mnemonic figure maps chemotherapy agents to their signature organ toxicities. The agent depicted as causing hemorrhagic cystitis produces a bladder-toxic metabolite; co-administration of which agent prevents this toxicity?

explanation.summary: Cyclophosphamide generates acrolein, which injures the bladder urothelium and causes hemorrhagic cystitis. Mesna binds and detoxifies acrolein in the urine. Leucovorin rescues methotrexate toxicity, amifostine protects against cisplatin nephro/ototoxicity, and dexrazoxane guards against anthracycline cardiotoxicity.
explanation.whyCorrect: Cyclophosphamide generates acrolein, which injures the bladder urothelium and causes hemorrhagic cystitis. Mesna binds and detoxifies acrolein in the urine. Leucovorin rescues methotrexate toxicity, amifostine protects against cisplatin nephro/ototoxicity, and dexrazoxane guards against anthracycline cardiotoxicity.
